author: Clement of Rome
book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

lineage: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
Imitating These Examples, Let Us Seek After Peace.

ccel.title: ANF09. The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Thus the humility and godly submission of so great and illustrious men have rendered not only us, but also all the generations before us, better; even as many as have received His oracles in fear and truth.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Wherefore, having so many great and glorious examples set before us, let us turn again to the practice of that peace which from the beginning was the mark set before us; and let us look stedfastly to the Father and Creator of the universe, and cleave to His mighty and surpassingly great gifts and benefactions of peace.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Let us contemplate Him with our understanding, and look with the eyes of our soul to His long-suffering will.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Let us reflect how free from the wrath He is towards all His creation.
